input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

input.description: Path to comma-separated file containing information about the samples in the experiment.
input.help_text: You will need to create a design file with information about the samples in your experiment before running the pipeline. Use this parameter to specify its location. It has to be a comma-separated file with 3 columns, and a header row.

adapters_fasta.description: Path to FASTA file containing adapter sequences to be trimmed

pon_cnn.description: Panel of normal reference to use for CNV calling with CNVkit.

baits.description: Path to BED or Interval List file containing location of baits.

targets.description: Path to BED or Interval List file containing location of targets.

target_padding.description: The number of bp by which to pad each target interval on either side (default = 100) for variant calling by GATK4/MUTECT2. Target-padding cannot be negative and will not exceed the bounds of the contig.

population_germline_vcf.description: Optional path to the compressed germline resource VCF file. Should contain allele frequencies.

pon_vcf.description: Optional path to the compressed panel of normals VCF file.

outdir.description: The output directory where the results will be saved. You have to use absolute paths to storage on Cloud infrastructure.

multiqc_title.description: MultiQC report title. Printed as page header, used for filename if not otherwise specified.

annotation_genome_version.description: The version of the genome that SnpEff and VEP will use for variant annotation. Defaults to GRCh38.

snpeff_cache.description: The location of the root of the annotation cache folder. You have to use absolute paths to storage on Cloud infrastructure (i.e., s3://my-reference-data/cache/snpeff_cache/).

snpeff_db.description: The database version that SnpEff will use for variant annotation. Alternatively databases' names can be listed with the snpEff databases. Defaults to version 99.

ensemblvep_species.description: The species for which to pull annotation information. Defaults to `homo sapiens`.
ensemblvep_species.help_text: Alternatively, species listed in Ensembl Genomes caches can be used for non-human genome annotaiton.

ensemblvep_cache.description: Path to VEP cache which should contain the relevant species, genome and build directories. You have to use absolute paths to storage on Cloud infrastructure (i.e., s3://my-reference-data/cache/vep_cache/).
ensemblvep_cache.help_text: The VEP cache stores information about regulatory regions, attributes about different variants and transcripts, as well as predictions and scores for SIFT and PolyPhen-2.

ensemblvep_cache_version.description: The version of the VEP cache to use for annotation. Defaults to version 114.
ensemblvep_cache_version.help_text: More information can be found here: https://useast.ensembl.org/info/docs/tools/vep/script/vep_cache.html.

cosmic_vcf.description: Path to a VCF file that has been emitted from COSMIC, compressed, and indexed.
cosmic_vcf.help_text:
To be used with VEP's `--custom` flag .
More information can be found here: https://useast.ensembl.org/info/docs/tools/vep/script/vep_custom.html

skip_civicpy.description: If true, skip the CIVICpy annotation step.

tmb_mutect2_config.description: Path to the variant calling config file that defines how pyTMB should interpret the VCF structure as emitted by GATK_MUTECT2.

tmb_snpeff_config.description: Path to the variant annotation config file that describes how pyTMB should parse functional annotations in a VCF annotated by SnpEff.

tool_selection_options.title: Tool selection options
tool_selection_options.description: Options related to selection of tools used in the workflow.

use_msisensor_pro_licensed.description: Enable if you are a licensed user of MSIsensor-pro to use MSIsensor-pro instead of MSIsensor2, https://github.com/xjtu-omics/msisensor-pro/blob/master/LICENSE.

reference_genome_options.title: Reference genome options
reference_genome_options.description: Reference genome related files and options required for the workflow.

fasta.help_text: This parameter is required. If you don't have a BWA index available this will be generated for you automatically. Combine with `--save_reference` to save BWA index for future runs.

dict.help_text: **NB** If none provided, will be generated automatically from the FASTA reference. Combine with `--save_reference` to save for future runs.

fasta_fai.help_text: **NB** If none provided, will be generated automatically from the FASTA reference. Combine with `--save_reference` to save for future runs.

fasta_gzi.help_text: **NB** If none provided, will be generated automatically from the FASTA reference. Combine with `--save_reference` to save for future runs.
fasta_gzi.description: Path to FASTA reference .gzi index for gzipped fasta reference only.

bwa.help_text: **NB** If none provided, will be generated automatically from the FASTA reference. Combine with `--save_reference` to save for future runs.

msisensor_scan.description: Path to msisensor2 (or msisensor-pro) scan file.
msisensor_scan.help_text: **NB** If none provided, will be generated automatically from the FASTA reference. Combine with `--save_reference` to save for future runs.

msisensor2_model_name.description: The specific model name for MSIsensor 2 analysis (default: hg38).

save_reference.description: If generated by the pipeline save the STAR index in the results directory.
save_reference.help_text: If an alignment index is generated by the pipeline use this parameter to save it to your results folder. These can then be used for future pipeline runs, reducing processing times.

generic_options.title: Generic options
generic_options.description: Less common options for the pipeline, typically set in a config file.
generic_options.help_text:
These options are common to all nf-core pipelines and allow you to customise some of the core preferences for how the pipeline runs.

Typically these options would be set in a Nextflow config file loaded for all pipeline runs, such as `~/.nextflow/config`.

publish_dir_mode.description: Method used to save pipeline results to output directory.
publish_dir_mode.help_text: The Nextflow `publishDir` option specifies which intermediate files should be saved to the output directory. This option tells the pipeline what method should be used to move these files. See [Nextflow docs](https://www.nextflow.io/docs/latest/process.html#publishdir) for details.

max_multiqc_email_size.description: File size limit when attaching MultiQC reports to summary emails.

monochrome_logs.description: Do not use coloured log outputs.

multiqc_config.description: Custom config file to supply to MultiQC.

multiqc_logo.description: Custom logo file to supply to MultiQC. File name must also be set in the MultiQC config file

validate_params.description: Boolean whether to validate parameters against the schema at runtime

pipelines_testdata_base_path.description: Base URL or local path to location of pipeline test dataset files

trace_report_suffix.description: Suffix to add to the trace report filename. Default is the date and time in the format yyyy-MM-dd_HH-mm-ss.
